expectations.text: Response identifies multiple underpricing signals from the scenario (no pushback, 'great deal' feedback, years without change)
expectations.evidence: Identifies: zero pushback as pricing signal, inflation erosion over 3 years, unpriced scope additions, market movement. Clear diagnosis.

expectations.text: Response recommends a specific price increase range or new price point
expectations.evidence: Existing clients: $6,500-$7,500/month (30-50% increase). New clients: $7,500-$10,000/month (50-100% increase).

expectations.text: Response recommends HOW to implement the increase (grandfather, delayed, restructure, etc.)
expectations.evidence: 60-90 day notice for existing, phase if needed (two-step), annual commitment incentive, immediate new pricing for prospects. Suggests 2-3 tiers.

expectations.text: Response connects the AI content generation and analytics dashboards to justifying the increase
expectations.evidence: Communication anchoring advice explicitly mentions AI content generation and analytics dashboards as key framing points.

expectations.text: Response addresses the importance of not setting-and-forgetting pricing
expectations.evidence: Mentions 'The best time to raise was a year ago. The second best time is now' and 'wait another year' as a risk, but does not frame this as an ongoing practice to revisit pricing regularly. No mention of periodic review cadence.

expectations.text: Response addresses how to mitigate risk of client churn from the increase
expectations.evidence: 'What Could Go Wrong' section addresses churn risk. Also mentions: 'Be prepared to lose a client or two. This is normal and healthy.'
